note: E6 (issue #7): implementation-linked evidence seeding the Kurtosis repro target. Curated from the dataset rows whose `label` matches a label used by our 01e properties (beacon-chain:justification-and-finality, beacon-chain:slashing, beacon-chain:effective-balance-updates) AND whose `pre_fix_code`/`files_changed` are non-empty — the pre-fix hunks pin the exact implementation site a devnet reproduction should target. Full hunks live in the dataset (fields pre_fix_code / post_fix_code / files_changed / fix_commit); only a capped excerpt of the first hunk is mirrored here. Selection is curated (label-matched + FFG-semantics-linked), not exhaustive: 37 dataset rows match our labels; rows whose fix is unrelated plumbing (dependency bumps, test-only, CLI deprecations) are not seeds.
match_rule: seed.label == property.label (attached only to properties that have a real Executable checker)

seeds.label: beacon-chain:justification-and-finality
seeds.title: Fix an overflow in the justification bits processing
seeds.pre_fix_code_excerpt:
# beacon_chain/spec/state_transition_epoch.nim @ line 200
  ## Spec:
  ## state.justification_bits[1:] = state.justification_bits[:-1]
  ## state.justification_bits[0] = 0b0
  state.justification_bits = state.justification_bits shl 1

  # This is a somewhat expensive approach
  let active_validator_indices =
seeds.why_implementation_linked: Justification-bits shift overflow in process_justification_and_finalization; a wrong bitfield diverges the justified/finalized checkpoint bookkeeping the justification/finality theorems (finalized_means_justified_child, k_finalized_means_justified) constrain.

seeds.label: beacon-chain:justification-and-finality
seeds.title: Fix deadlock in finalization migration
seeds.why_implementation_linked: Deadlock in the finalization migration path; stalls the finalized-checkpoint advance whose consistency the justification/finality properties audit.

seeds.label: beacon-chain:slashing
seeds.title: Delete temporary mitigation for equivocation via RPC
seeds.why_implementation_linked: Equivocation-via-RPC mitigation path; equivocation detection is exactly the S1 double-vote predicate slashedB decides (slashed_double_vote_iff_bex).

seeds.label: beacon-chain:slashing
seeds.title: annotate slashing protection v2 with uint64 -> int64 overflow conditions
seeds.pre_fix_code_excerpt:
# beacon_chain/validators/slashing_protection_v2.nim @ line 720
  block:
    # Condition 1 at https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-3076
    var root: ETH2Digest
    let status = db.sqlBlockForSameSlot.exec(
          (valID, int64 slot)
        ) do (res: Hash32):
seeds.why_implementation_linked: uint64 -> int64 overflow conditions in slashing-protection v2 (EIP-3076 conditions); numeric-representation divergence in the S1/S2 slashing predicates slashedB decides. Same numeric class as findings_map lodestar-slashing-uint64-as-js-number.

seeds.label: beacon-chain:effective-balance-updates
seeds.title: Fix total_effective_balance=0 in `PreEpochCache`
seeds.why_implementation_linked: total_effective_balance=0 in PreEpochCache / get_total_active_balance; weight arithmetic feeding the 2/3-quorum threshold that quorum_2 / q_intersection_slashed (qIntersectionWitnessB) depend on.

seeds.label: beacon-chain:effective-balance-updates
seeds.title: Guard against unsafe numbers
seeds.why_implementation_linked: Guard against unsafe JS numbers in totalActiveBalanceByIncrement; effective- balance weight representation feeding the quorum threshold arithmetic.
